name: Maigret
description: Collect a dossier on a person by username across 3000+ sites.
bestFor: Enumerating accounts tied to a username/nickname and extracting profile data
trustNote: Flagship username-search tool (33k stars), actively maintained by soxoj; successor/expansion of Sherlock with profile parsing.
notes: pip install maigret or run via Docker. Checks 3000+ sites, parses profile pages for tags, and can output HTML/PDF dossiers. Great first pass when you only have a handle. Use --timeout and --top-sites to manage noise; some hits are false positives so verify manually.

name: SpiderFoot
description: Automates OSINT collection across 200+ modules for threat intel and attack-surface mapping.
bestFor: Automated correlation of emails, names, domains, IPs, usernames into a graph
trustNote: Widely used, mature OSINT automation engine (18k stars). Open-source core; SpiderFoot HX is the paid hosted version.
notes: Run locally with a web UI (python sf.py -l 127.0.0.1:5001). Many modules need API keys. Most modules are infra/threat-intel focused, but email, name, phone, and account modules are useful for people work. Note: poppopjmp/spiderfoot is a community fork.

name: OSINT Framework (lockfale)
description: Web-based directory tree of OSINT tools and resources organized by data type.
bestFor: Browsing categorized links to tools by selector (username, email, phone, etc.)
trustNote: The canonical osintframework.com directory (11.5k stars); a curated link tree, not a tool that fetches data itself.
notes: Hosted at osintframework.com. A node-tree of links pointing to external tools/sites; use it to discover tools per data type. Itself a frontier source to mine for more candidates.

name: Sn1per
description: Automated penetration testing and attack-surface management platform with recon, scan, exploit.
bestFor: Automated network/domain reconnaissance and attack-surface enumeration
trustNote: Popular pentest/recon platform (10k stars); free community edition plus paid Professional.
notes: Active scanning tool aimed at infrastructure, not people. Low relevance to missing-persons casework; included for completeness.

name: BBOT
description: Recursive internet scanner that chains modules for subdomains, emails, and attack surface.
bestFor: Recursive domain/email/subdomain enumeration
trustNote: Maintained by Black Lantern Security (9.9k stars); reputable infosec tooling.
notes: pipx install bbot. Primarily infra recon; the email/username/social modules can occasionally surface a person's accounts but it is not a people-search tool.

name: Mr.Holmes
description: All-in-one OSINT tool for usernames, emails, phones, and domain info.
bestFor: Quick multi-selector lookups (username, phone, email, domain) from one CLI
trustNote: Popular community OSINT multi-tool (3.6k stars); maintained by an individual.
notes: Python CLI/GUI bundling phone (PhoneInfoga-style), username, and Google-dorking modules. Results quality varies by module; verify hits. Works on Linux/Termux.

name: GoSearch
description: Search anyone's digital footprint across 300+ websites by username.
bestFor: Fast username enumeration with breach/HudsonRock integration
trustNote: Fast Go-based username searcher (3.4k stars); active community project.
notes: go install or download a release binary. Faster than Sherlock; integrates HudsonRock infostealer data and breach directory lookups. Good complement to Maigret.

name: sn0int
description: Semi-automatic OSINT framework and package manager with scriptable modules.
bestFor: Scriptable, repeatable OSINT workflows over a local entity database
trustNote: Well-regarded Rust OSINT framework by kpcyrd (2.4k stars).
notes: Modules are Lua scripts installed from a registry. Stores findings in a local SQLite entity graph. Supports geolocation, wifi/BSSID, domains, accounts. Steeper learning curve but powerful for structured investigations.

name: Tookie-OSINT
description: Advanced OSINT tool that finds social media accounts based on inputs.
bestFor: Finding social media accounts from a username
notes: Python tool similar to Sherlock/Maigret; configurable site list. Verify hits manually as account-finders produce false positives.

name: Harpoon
description: CLI tool for open-source and threat intelligence with many plugin commands.
bestFor: Threat-intel-flavored OSINT lookups (domains, IPs, some social platforms)
trustNote: Mature CLI by Te-k (1.3k stars); broad plugin set.
notes: pip install harpoon. Many commands need API keys. Mostly CTI/infra focused; has Twitter/Telegram/Instagram plugins that can help on the people side.

name: osint-tools (HowToFind-bot)
description: Catalog of open-source OSINT tools.
bestFor: Discovering additional OSINT tools by category
notes: A curated list rather than a tool. Frontier source for further harvesting.

name: socid-extractor
description: Extracts structured account data (IDs, names, bios) from profile URLs across 150+ sites.
bestFor: Pulling hidden account IDs and metadata from a known profile URL
trustNote: By soxoj (Maigret author); the extraction engine behind Maigret (1k stars).
notes: pip install socid-extractor. Give it a profile URL and it returns structured fields (internal user IDs, registration dates, linked emails). Excellent for pivoting from one profile to another via shared IDs.

name: seekr
description: Multi-purpose OSINT toolkit with a web interface for organizing investigations.
bestFor: Case management plus username/phone/email lookups in a web UI
trustNote: Active community OSINT toolkit with notebook-style case tracking (816 stars).
notes: Go-based, runs a local web server. Combines lookups with a notes/case-tracking system, which is genuinely useful for a structured missing-persons investigation.

name: OSINT-SAN
description: Framework for rapid information discovery and user deanonymization.
bestFor: Multi-module deanonymization (IP, phone, social, geolocation)
trustNote: Community deanonymization framework (604 stars); some modules use grabber/IP-logger techniques (active).
notes: Includes IP-logger/grabber modules that interact with targets (active opsec). Use the passive modules only for casework. PRO variant exists (Bafomet666/OSINT-SAN-PRO).

name: DarkScrape
description: OSINT tool for scraping dark websites (.onion).
bestFor: Crawling/scraping Tor hidden services
trustNote: Community dark-web scraper (508 stars); older project.
notes: Requires Tor. Niche; low relevance unless a case touches dark-web content.

name: DFW1N-OSINT
description: Australia-focused open-source intelligence resource repository.
bestFor: Australian public records, registries, and people resources
trustNote: Curated AU-centric OSINT link repository (416 stars).
notes: A curated regional resource list, valuable when a case has an Australian nexus. Frontier source for AU-specific registries.

name: Oryon querytool
description: Google-Spreadsheet-based OSINT framework for complex searches of people, emails, files.
bestFor: Dork-driven searches for people, emails, usernames, and files via a spreadsheet
notes: A Google Sheet that builds dork URLs across many engines/sites for a given term. Requires a Google account. Handy for systematic name/email/username searching.

name: H.I.V.E
description: Automated OSINT multi-tool for efficient data gathering.
bestFor: One-shot automated recon across multiple selectors
notes: Python automation wrapper around common OSINT lookups. Quality depends on bundled modules; verify.

name: GhostRecon
description: OSINT framework for online investigations.
bestFor: General-purpose multi-selector online investigation
notes: General investigation toolkit; overlaps heavily with other multi-tools. Verify module sources before trusting output.

name: TwitWork
description: Monitor a Twitter stream from a Node.js Electron desktop app.
bestFor: Live monitoring of Twitter/X streams
trustNote: Community Electron Twitter monitor (272 stars); likely affected by Twitter/X API changes.
notes: Needs Twitter API credentials; post-API-changes functionality may be limited. Situational for social monitoring.

name: Coeus-OSINT-ToolBox
description: OSINT toolbox bundling tools for intelligence gathering from open sources.
bestFor: GUI-driven multi-selector OSINT lookups
notes: Python GUI toolbox aggregating several modules. Verify individual module reliability.

name: ogi
description: Open-source link analysis and OSINT framework.
bestFor: Graph/link analysis of entities (Maltego-style, open source)
notes: Visual link-analysis tool useful for mapping relationships between entities in a case.

name: Maltego transforms (M0m0SMS-OSINT)
description: Compilation of Maltego assets, local transforms, and helper scripts.
bestFor: Extending Maltego graph analysis with community transforms
trustNote: Community collection of Maltego transforms (155 stars); requires Maltego (freemium) to use.
notes: Not standalone; these are local transforms/assets for Maltego (Maltego CE is free with limits). Useful for graph-based entity linking in investigations.

name: Cyber Intelligence Toolkit (oryon)
description: Curated manuals, playbooks, and checklists for OSINT, OPSEC, and investigations.
bestFor: Methodology, playbooks, and investigator checklists
notes: Reference material, not a tool. Useful for structuring an investigation workflow and OPSEC.

name: osintBOX
description: Script to provision a Parrot OS distro with popular OSINT tools.
bestFor: Standing up an OSINT-ready VM/distro quickly
trustNote: Community provisioning script for Parrot OS (120 stars).
notes: Installer/setup script, not an investigative tool. Useful for building an investigator workstation.

name: IntelHub (Chrome extension)
description: Chrome extension bringing OSINT tools, metadata analyzers, and Google dorking into the browser.
bestFor: In-browser access to OSINT tools, dork builders, and metadata checks
notes: Browser-based launcher/quick-access for many OSINT resources and dork templates. Convenient for manual investigations.

name: instatracker
description: Instagram tracking script that logs changes to an account (followers, following, posts, bio).
bestFor: Monitoring an Instagram account over time for changes
trustNote: Community Instagram change-logger (102 stars) by gosearch author.
notes: Logs follower/following/post/bio changes for a target Instagram account. Useful when monitoring a known social profile during an active case; subject to Instagram rate limits/blocks.

name: One-Liner-OSINT
description: Collection of powerful one-liner commands for OSINT gathering.
bestFor: Copy-paste shell one-liners for quick OSINT tasks
notes: A cheat-sheet of shell commands rather than a tool. Mostly infra-leaning.

name: OSINTBookmarks
description: OSINT bookmarks file for Firefox/Chrome/Edge/Safari.
bestFor: Importing a large curated set of OSINT site bookmarks into a browser
notes: Importable bookmarks of OSINT resources. A frontier/discovery aid rather than a tool.

name: OSINT-Tools (yogsec)
description: Collection of OSINT tools and methods for intelligence gathering.
bestFor: Discovering OSINT tools and methods by category
notes: Curated list; frontier source for further harvesting.

name: Osint-Sync
description: Browser extension for OSINT (Osint Sync).
bestFor: In-browser quick access to OSINT resources
notes: Browser extension launcher for OSINT sites. Convenience tool.

name: osint-framework (osint-dev-team)
description: All-in-one OSINT-RECON Swiss Knife.
bestFor: Bundled multi-selector recon automation
notes: Dockerized recon framework; infra-leaning. Verify modules.

name: GhostIntel
description: Python OSINT framework for investigation via username, email, domain, IP, and phone.
bestFor: Multi-selector lookups (username, email, domain, IP, phone) from one CLI
notes: Lightweight Python multi-tool. Overlaps with larger tools; useful as a quick all-in-one.

name: osint-investigation (coldvisionz)
description: Collection of useful OSINT tools.
bestFor: Discovering investigation tools by category
notes: Curated list; frontier source.

name: OSINTLAB
description: Script that automates installation of ~50 OSINT recon tools.
bestFor: Bulk-installing common OSINT tools on a workstation
notes: Installs ~50 tools; a setup helper, not an investigative tool. Frontier: the bundled list points to many more tools.

name: DIGI-NETRA
description: Python toolkit for tracing phone numbers, usernames, IPs, and emails via CLI.
bestFor: CLI tracing of phone/username/IP/email
notes: Multi-selector tracer; phone geolocation is carrier/region level only. Verify results.

name: cybersecurity-osint (paulveillard)
description: Curated collection of OSINT tools, frameworks, and resources.
bestFor: Discovering OSINT tools and frameworks by topic
notes: Curated list; frontier source for more candidates.

name: one (charliebrassington)
description: OSINT tool with multiple scrapers including a cyberbackgroundchecks integration.
bestFor: US people-search scraping (background-check style data)
trustNote: Small community scraper (34 stars); scrapes third-party people-search sites (ToS-sensitive).
notes: Scrapes cyberbackgroundchecks and similar US people-search sites to return addresses, phones, and relatives. High relevance for US missing-persons leads, but scraping these sites may violate ToS and data can be stale; verify.

name: gitsint-rs
description: Platform for uncovering connections and exposed secrets across GitHub.
bestFor: Finding emails/identities linked to a GitHub account
trustNote: Community GitHub-OSINT tool in Rust (33 stars).
notes: Pivots from a GitHub username to associated emails (via commit metadata) and connected accounts. Useful when a subject has a developer footprint.

name: intel-codex
description: Knowledge base covering OSINT, forensics, reverse engineering, and security frameworks.
bestFor: Methodology and reference reading for OSINT/forensics
notes: Reference material, not a tool.

name: OSINT-Tool-For-TG
description: Free tool for gathering OSINT from Telegram and other platforms.
bestFor: Telegram user/group intelligence gathering
notes: Needs Telegram API credentials. Telegram can resolve a phone number to a username/account if the target uses Telegram. Situational but valuable for messaging-linked leads.

name: Probe_spider
description: Python OSINT tool for reconnaissance.
bestFor: General reconnaissance automation
notes: Infra-leaning recon tool.

name: OSRFramework (jaykali fork)
description: Framework of APIs and tools for accurate online research (usernames, domains, mails).
bestFor: Username/email/domain searches via usufy/mailfy/searchfy utilities
trustNote: Fork of the well-known i3visio OSRFramework (23 stars on this fork; original is widely used).
notes: Classic OSINT suite: usufy (usernames), mailfy (emails), searchfy (full names), phonefy (phones), domainfy. Original i3visio repo may be unmaintained; this is a fork. Solid for username/email enumeration despite age.

name: Arina OSINT nickname checker
description: Tool for discovering information about historical online accounts/pages by nickname.
bestFor: Checking a nickname across sites, including historical pages
notes: Username enumeration with some archived/historical page lookups. Verify hits.

name: OSINT-Explorer
description: OSINT framework for intelligence gathering.
bestFor: General-purpose OSINT gathering
notes: General OSINT framework; overlaps with larger tools.

name: osint-vm (Inforensics)
description: Virtual machine for OSINT investigators with AI tools and agents.
bestFor: Pre-built investigator VM with AI-assisted OSINT tooling
notes: An investigator VM image/config, not a standalone tool. Useful for workstation setup with AI agents.

name: karus
description: Multi-purpose OSINT utility with a custom web interface.
bestFor: Web-UI multi-selector OSINT lookups
notes: Web-interface OSINT toolkit. Verify modules.

name: pinosin
description: Framework for investigating individuals and tracking investigation progress.
bestFor: Person-centric investigation with case tracking
notes: Focused on individuals with progress tracking; conceptually aligned with missing-persons workflows. Small/young project.

name: zer0sint
description: Tool to uncover and examine publicly accessible information.
bestFor: General public-info lookups across selectors
notes: General-purpose lookups; overlaps with larger tools.

name: Osint-ToolKit (ayxkaddd)
description: Web interface providing access to various OSINT tools and resources.
bestFor: Web-UI access to multiple OSINT lookups
trustNote: Small community web OSINT toolkit (15 stars).
notes: Modern web UI aggregating OSINT tools. Verify modules.

name: Recon-ng (paralax fork)
description: Reconnaissance-focused OSINT framework (personal fork).
bestFor: Modular recon with marketplace modules (contacts, profiles, hosts)
trustNote: Recon-ng is a well-known, mature framework; this is a personal fork (15 stars). Prefer the canonical lanmaster53/recon-ng.
notes: Metasploit-style modular OSINT framework. Modules for contacts, profiles, hosts; many need API keys. Use the upstream lanmaster53/recon-ng rather than this fork. Contacts/profiles modules help on the people side.

name: Telegram-OSINT-2022
description: Curated collection of Telegram-focused OSINT resources.
bestFor: Discovering Telegram OSINT tools and methods
notes: Resource list, not a tool. Frontier source for Telegram-specific tooling.

name: Open-Source-INTelligence (txuswashere)
description: Comprehensive list of OSINT tools and methodologies.
bestFor: Broad discovery of OSINT tools and methods
notes: Curated list; frontier source for further harvesting.

name: IntelBox (malwaredojo)
description: Installer arsenal of OSINT tools for Debian systems and VMs.
bestFor: Bulk-installing OSINT tools on Debian/VM
notes: Setup helper; bundled list is a frontier for more tools.
